text.title: vanilla-extract Zero-Runtime CSS
text.summary: vanilla-extract produces plain CSS at build time via recipe(), createVar(), styleVariants(), and Sprinkles. All .css.ts files run in a Node sandbox — no window, process.env, or dynamic imports.

rule.statement: Use createVar() to declare CSS custom properties and recipe() to combine base styles with typed variant maps. Always set defaultVariants.
rule.rationale: createVar() produces a named CSS variable with TypeScript type; omitting defaultVariants causes runtime errors when the recipe receives undefined for a required variant.

rule.statement: Treat Sprinkles properties like a public API. Audit generated CSS size in CI. Every added property compiles to N classnames where N = number of values × number of conditions.
rule.rationale: Over-broad Sprinkles property arrays expand CSS output by 5-10x; the resulting stylesheet causes measurable parse-time LCP regressions on mobile.

rule.statement: Keep .css.ts files dependency-free of app code. Only import other .css.ts files, types, and literal constants. Never reference window, process.env, or dynamic imports.
rule.rationale: vanilla-extract runs .css.ts in a restricted Node sandbox at build time; importing app code that references browser globals crashes the build cryptically.
